mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS falls or stays flat after prior tightening, equity discount rates ease and risk appetite improves, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if policy remains restrictive while inflation is still rising.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered easing observations do not outperform the unconditional 20d mean, or if 20d forward returns are negative in more than 55% of periods with falling FEDFUNDS and stable CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A labor-market deterioration shock, measured by a rising UNRATE, signals weaker household income and demand, which pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when employment also stops growing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances after a >0.3pp 3-month UNRATE rise, or if Rank IC over triggered periods is above 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS indicates stronger retail demand and better near-term revenue visibility for U.S. equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal fails when sales growth is driven by inflation rather than real demand.
failure_condition: Reject if post-trigger 5d returns are not above baseline, or if the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds RSAFS growth for 2 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent improvement in INDPRO and HOUST signals broadening real activity and easing capacity constraints, which supports S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is falsified when industrial output rises but housing collapses, indicating sector-specific distortion rather than economy-wide strength.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-signal state does not deliver higher 60d forward returns than either proxy alone, or if the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 below its recent trend signals weaker aggregate final demand and lower expected corporate earnings, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when GDP weakness is offset by simultaneous disinflation and easier policy.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 real growth below 2-quarter moving average
failure_condition: Reject if 60d forward returns are not negative in growth-slowdown windows, or if the sign flips positive when FEDFUNDS is already falling and CPIAUCSL is decelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% after inflation has not fallen enough signals restrictive real rates, which depress equity multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect weakens once inflation is clearly falling.
failure_condition: Reject if 5d forward returns after FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not below baseline, or if the signal vanishes when CPIAUCSL is already declining for 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth remains positive while UNRATE is flat or lower, the labor market is still absorbing demand, which supports consumer spending and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if job growth is accompanied by falling labor-force participation pressure not captured here.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and nonrising UNRATE does not outperform the unconditional sample, or if 20d returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising INDPRO combined with stable or rising HOUST marks normalization of supply capacity and construction activity, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if industrial gains are accompanied by housing weakness and no broad cyclical rebound.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction does not improve 60d predictive accuracy versus INDPRO alone, or if the joint recovery state fails to generate positive excess returns over a 3-year rolling test.
